title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. vulgatiflorum
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. vulgatiflorum is a subspecies of the hawkweed plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) known for its yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Hieracium vulgatum subsp. vulgatiflorum include its potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
